summary: PQ-Aliro makes genuine engineering contributions but has a fatal structural flaw: §7 is a placeholder. Additionally protocol diagram Fig. 1 omits LOADCERT round, reader_identifier is transmitted in plaintext contradicting tracking-resilience claim, and UD_noise is absent from key derivation formula.
strengths: First complete PQC upgrade of Aliro NFC protocol
Android HCE key isolation and asynchronous signing insights
Well-structured security model §6 with explicit trivial attack enumeration
Open-source implementation with in-memory test harness
weaknesses: CRITICAL: §7 one placeholder sentence only—formal proof entirely absent
MAJOR: Fig 1 protocol diagram omits LOADCERT round—formal model is for different protocol than implemented
MAJOR: reader_identifier transmitted in plaintext in AUTH0—contradicts tracking-resilience goal
MAJOR: UD_noise not in HKDF derivation formula in Fig 1—UD contributes no fresh randomness per figure
MAJOR: Blindness/strong one-wayness definitions in §4 never used—appear to be from different paper
SIGNIFICANT: 'perfect forward secrecy' overclaimed—paper explicitly excludes ephemeral state compromise, achieving only BR-style FS
SIGNIFICANT: Certificate revocation not addressed for key_slot optimisation
MODERATE: ~40 unused bib entries (subversion attacks, PAKE, reverse firewalls) never cited—suggests copied bibliography
MODERATE: NFC relay attacks not discussed—relevant to physical access control
MODERATE: CCC Digital Key 3.0 comparison superficial

detailed_comments.comment: Contains only: 'We prove that PQ-Aliro is secure in the Bellare-Rogaway-style AKE security model.' No theorem, no reduction, no proof. Abstract and introduction prominently advertise formal proof as Contribution 3.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Either provide full proof or proof sketch with theorem statement and reductions; or reframe as 'security argument' and remove 'prove' claims throughout

detailed_comments.comment: Protocol diagram shows 2-round post-KEM exchange (Reader sends sigma+cert, UD responds with c_UD). Does not show LOADCERT as separate round. LOADCERT accounts for ~5.5KB and ~22 APDUs—entirely absent from formal specification.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Revise Fig 1 to show AUTH0/LOADCERT/AUTH1 as three separate rounds, or explicitly justify the abstraction

detailed_comments.comment: reader_identifier (32 bytes) transmitted in plaintext in AUTH0 Command. Stable linkable identifier allows passive eavesdropper to track all sessions at a reader. TestPrivacy experiment only tests UD identity, not reader identity. Contradicts tracking-resilience goal.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Acknowledge one-sided tracking resilience in §10 Limitations; or propose session-specific reader pseudonym mechanism

detailed_comments.comment: UD_noise (32B) in AUTH0 Response per §3 and communication-overhead.md, but HKDF derivation in Fig 1 uses only trans=H(pk_e||c_e). UD contributes no fresh randomness to session key per formal protocol.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Clarify whether UD_noise feeds into HKDF derivation; update Fig 1 if so; explain security role of UD_noise

detailed_comments.comment: Paper claims 'perfect forward secrecy' but §6 explicitly states ephemeral state compromise excluded from model. Achieves only BR-style FS (long-term key corruption), not eCK-style FS.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Replace 'perfect forward secrecy' with 'forward secrecy (under long-term key corruption)'; add remark distinguishing from eCK notion

detailed_comments.comment: key_slot optimisation caches Reader certificates on UD. Certificate revocation not discussed. Compromised/decommissioned reader certificates cached on UD will grant continued access.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Add certificate revocation discussion to §10: OCSP, CRLs, or online checks; analyse feasibility within NFC bandwidth constraints

detailed_comments.comment: No discussion of NFC relay attacks (Hancke-Kuhn 2005, Drimer-Murdoch 2007)—well-known threat to NFC physical access control. transaction_identifier provides replay protection not relay protection.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Add relay attack paragraph to §2 or §10; cite foundational relay attack papers; clarify PQ-Aliro relies on NFC physical 10cm range limit

detailed_comments.comment: ~40 bib entries never cited (subversion attacks, PAKE, reverse firewalls, kleptography). Vestigial ECC notation (cyclic group G, generator g, prime p) never used.
detailed_comments.suggestion: Clean bib file to include only cited entries; remove ECC group notation from §4

missing_citations.claim: ISO/IEC 14443 APDU frame structure and bit rates
Tracking resilience / anonymity in contactless protocols
missing_citations.suggested_reference_type: Vaudenay RFID privacy work; Juels-Rivest-Szydlo yoking proofs

missing_citations.claim: Grover halves symmetric security -> AES-256 adequate
missing_citations.suggested_reference_type: NIST IR 8105; Mosca quantum risk timeline
